calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-12-2016 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHANIE M. PROFIT, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-4492 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NICOLE E. PARSONS OF THE BERG INJURY LAWYERS, TEL. NO. 1-510-523-3200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Summary Adjudication -
Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted as to the Berg Defendants and denied as to defendant Tina Jones. The City satisfied its initial burden by presenting evidence that the Berg Defendants are liable for conversion and breach of constructive trustees' duties under Health Code section 124.5(e). Health Code section 124 provides for an automatic lien on the settlement amount which the Berg Defendants assisted Ms. Jones in obtaining. The evidence shows that a settlement check in the amount of $15,000 was issued to Berg Injury Lawyers and Ms. Jones, yet the City has not received any payment for the medical services and treatment it rendered to Ms. Jones, The Berg Defendants did not present any evidence creating a triable issue. Their arguments that Health Code sections 124 and 124.5 are preempted by Government Code section 23004.1 and violate the State Bar Act were rejected by the June 26, 2015 order overruling their demurrer to the second and third causes of action. The Berg Defendants' request that the common-fund doctrine be applied to limit Plaintiff's recovery is denied. (City and County of San Francisco v. Sweet (1995) 12 Cal.4th 105, 108, fn. 1.) Because Ms. Jones has not appeared in the case her default was entered on July 20, 2015, summary judgment is not appropriate as to her. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Continued To Jan-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-18-2015 Continued To Dec-15-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tara M. Steeley, of Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4700, for plaintiffs;
Robert Dalby, of Berg Injury Lawyers, Tel. 510.523.3200, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS' WILLIAM GINSBERG, BERG INJURY LAWYERS, INC., ROBERT DALBY, AND WILLIAM BERG'S DEMURRER PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION is overruled. Through its police power the City and County of San Francisco has the power to "make and enforce within its limits all local, police, sanitary, and other ordinances and regulations not in conflict with general laws." (Cal. Const., art XI, Sec. 7.) Sec. 124 and 124.5 of the San Francisco Health Code is not duplicative and does not conflict with Government Code Section 23004.1, therefore the City ordinance is not preempted. Further, because Section 124.5(c) requires "the recipient's attorney retained to assert the action or claim shall provide written notice of such action or claim by personal delivery or first-class mail to the Bureau of Delinquent Revenue Collection in the Office of the Treasurer-Tax Collector within 10 days of asserting such action or claim" the ordinance does not violate the State Bar Act because no privilege or duty of confidentiality applies to information that has already been disclosed. [See Cal. Evid. Code Sec. 912, subd. (a).]. Order signed in open court. Judge Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/SB)
